figures.caption: Figure 2: Low-dimensional geometric diagnostics of learned flows. Left (trajectory comparison): baseline FM paths exhibit stronger bending and non-uniform directional changes, while Iso-FM trajectories are more linear between source and target regions. Right (curvature comparison): the Iso-FM model maintains consistently lower curvature magnitude across integration time, empirically supporting the theoretical objective of acceleration suppression.

Figure 1: Teaser: Iso-FM suppresses path bending in low-dimensional transport. Left: baseline FM trajectories are more curved than Iso-FM trajectories. Right: Iso-FM yields lower curvature, consistent with reducing the material acceleration Dv/Dt.
